summary: DHH criticizes Danish government-funded organizations for exclusively adopting Microsoft's operating system, effectively acting as enforcers of Microsoft's monopoly. He frames this as worse than Microsoft directly suppressing competitors, since public institutions are using taxpayer money to entrench a single vendor's dominance.
key_points: Government-funded organizations choosing Microsoft exclusively amounts to state-sponsored monopoly enforcement
Public institutions shouldn't live in a 'fairy-tale world' where only one operating system exists
Microsoft benefits from monopoly reinforcement without having to do the 'dirty work' itself
money_quotes: Why bother doing the dirty work of "cutting off the air supply" of your competitors, when you can have state-sponsored organizations do it for you?
An organization funded by the Danish government has decided to live in the fairy-tale world of the single holy, hobgoblin operating system.
key_insight: When government institutions mandate a single vendor's technology, they become unwitting enforcers of that vendor's monopoly using public funds.
